section_label: Rights — Economic, Property, Labor & Social Security

variables.description: Whether the government can expropriate private property under at least some conditions
The specified level of compensation for expropriation of private property (asked only if EXPROP=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Fair/just; 2=Full (actual/market/current value); 3=Appropriate (reasonable); 4=Adequate; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for a right to own property
Whether the constitution uses the words 'socio-economic rights' or similar
Whether the constitution provides the right to just remuneration, fair or equal payment for work
Whether the constitution provides for the right to form or to join trade unions (must be explicit, not implied by freedom of association; includes occupational and professional associations)
Whether the constitution provides for a right to strike
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=Yes, but with limitations; 3=No; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for a right of rest and leisure (includes right to vacations, weekly holidays, and leaves with pay)
Whether the constitution provides for a right to an adequate or reasonable standard of living (synonyms: 'adequate well-being', 'suitable existence', 'life worthy of a human being')
Whether the constitution refers to the social security of the society or nation (general societal security/well-being, not a specific pension scheme)
Whether the constitution provides for general or financial support by the government for any of the following groups. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): FINSUP_1=Elderly; FINSUP_2=Unemployed; FINSUP_3=Disabled; FINSUP_4=Children and orphans; FINSUP_90=Left to non-constitutional law; FINSUP_96=Other; FINSUP_97=Unable to Determine; FINSUP_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for the right to choose one's occupation
Whether the constitution mentions the right to safe or healthy working conditions
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes (right of citizens); 2=No; 3=State duty to provide safe working conditions (only if explicitly a duty, not a right); 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution places limits on child employment (includes protections against exploitation or protections for 'minor workers')
Whether the constitution provides for the right to shelter or housing
Whether the constitution mentions a state duty to provide work or employment (distinct from right to work or right to choose occupation)
Whether the constitution mentions a state duty to provide health care
Whether the constitution provides the right to a free and/or competitive market
variables.notes: Borderline; indicates constitutional commitment to market economy
